description: MCP server for WebPrinter cloud printing. Supports environment checks, printer discovery, printer capability queries, local file upload, roaming print task creation, roaming task configuration updates, and direct printing to target devices.

schemes.name: WEBPRINTER_ACCESS_TOKEN
schemes.description: Pass the WebPrinter access token in the Authorization header as a Bearer token.

instructions: A valid WebPrinter access token is required before calling this MCP server. Get the token from https://any.webprinter.cn/get-ai-server-token and send it in the Authorization header as Bearer <token>. Recommended workflow: first call check_install_progress, then query_printers or query_printer_detail, upload local files with upload_file when needed, create roaming tasks with create_roaming_task, and finally adjust task settings with update_printer_side, update_printer_color, update_printer_copies, or update_printer_paper. Use direct_print_document only when the target printer and control_sn are already known.

tools.name: check_install_progress
tools.title: Check Install Progress
tools.description: Check whether the current account, client, and device environment are ready for WebPrinter cloud printing. Recommended as the first tool to call during setup or troubleshooting.

tools.name: query_printers
tools.title: Query Printers
tools.description: List printers available to the current user, including printers that can be used for direct printing or roaming task workflows.

tools.name: query_printer_detail
tools.title: Query Printer Detail
tools.description: Query detailed capabilities and metadata for a specific printer or shared device. Use this when printer-level details are needed before printing.

tools.name: upload_file
tools.title: Upload File
tools.description: Upload a local file and return a public URL that the print service can read. Use this before create_roaming_task or direct_print_document when the source file is not already hosted online.

file_path.description: Absolute or resolvable local file path.

tools.name: create_roaming_task
tools.title: Create Roaming Task
tools.description: Create a roaming print task from a public document URL. Returns a task identifier that can later be used to update side, color, copies, or paper settings.

tools.name: update_printer_side
tools.title: Update Printer Side
tools.description: Update simplex or duplex settings for an existing roaming print task. Suitable when the user asks for single-sided, duplex long-edge, or duplex short-edge printing.

tools.name: update_printer_color
tools.title: Update Printer Color
tools.description: Update color mode for an existing roaming print task. Suitable when the user asks for color or monochrome printing.

tools.name: update_printer_copies
tools.title: Update Printer Copies
tools.description: Update copy count for an existing roaming print task. Use when the user requests a specific number of printed copies.

tools.name: update_printer_paper
tools.title: Update Printer Paper
tools.description: Update paper size for an existing roaming print task using a standard preset like A3, A4, A5, LETTER, or custom width and height in millimeters.

tools.name: direct_print_document
tools.title: Direct Print Document
tools.description: Send a document directly to a specific printer when the target device name and control serial number are already known. If the printer is hidden and only supports roaming tasks, direct printing should not be used.

notes: Use upload_file before printing when the source document is local.
Roaming task updates require an existing task_id returned by create_roaming_task.
Paper presets such as A3 and A4 are automatically converted to millimeter width and height values.
A valid Bearer token is required for every request.
